mapping.a_element: the metric graph of bonds and vertices being quantized
mapping.b_element: the graph on which the Schroedinger operator is defined

mapping.a_element: the quantum spectrum of the operator on the graph
mapping.b_element: the spectrum of the Schroedinger operator on the graph

brief_justification: Both study the spectrum of a quantum/Schroedinger operator on a graph, but their transferable machinery differs: paper A derives an exact semiclassical trace formula expressing the spectrum as a sum over exponentially proliferating classical periodic orbits, linking spectral statistics to random matrix theory and chaotic dynamics, whereas paper B uses an exact spectral duality that reduces the continuous graph Schroedinger operator to a discrete Jacobi (difference) matrix of Kronig-Penney/Maryland type. Beyond the shared 'operator on a graph' setting, no role-for-role correspondence between periodic-orbit/RMT analysis and operator-to-Jacobi-matrix reduction holds, so they do not share an underlying method.
